learning_outcomes:
1. Major Programme:Through their training in anthropology, students will:(1) understand the world’s cultural diversity and human
universals;(2) know about anthropology’s subfields, and be able to think
using their ideas;(3) know and be able to think using data and methods from
archaeology;(4) comprehend and be able to apply the different theories
explaining sociocultural phenomena;(5) be able to design and carry out projects using anthropological
tools andperspectives; and(6)
be able, using anthropological modes of understanding, to think critically and
perceptively about one's society and the world. 2. Minor Programmes:(i) Anthropology (ii) Archaeology(iii) Cultural Heritage StudiesUpon the completion of the Minor Programme, students will have
demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject,
acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and
at a suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects
or to pursue a related career.

Anthropology Anthropology Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 69 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Any
three courses from:
ANTH1020# (recommended), ARTS1005, 1010, BMBL1001, CHES1101 or
1102, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1110, 1400 [CUMT1000],
ENGE1000, FAAS1900, HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701, JASP1090, LING1000,
MUSC1000, PHIL1110, THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  ANTH1010, 1100, 1710, 2110, 2111, 2200,
2210, 3380, 3630  |  27
(b)  |  Research
components (field project I and II):  |  6
ANTH4300,
4301
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
Nine courses from (a) and/or (b)
below, with at least four courses from (b) Ethnography and Area Studies:
(a)  |  ANTH1310, 2230, 2310, 2320, 2321, 2340, 2350, 2351, 2360, 2380,
2390, 2510, 2520, 2530, 2540, 2550, 2730, 2740, 2810, 3310, 3321, 3322,
3323, 3340, 3350, 3360, 3370, 3390, 3540, 3770, 3780, 3820[2820], 4210, 4220, 4321, 4330, 4410, 4720,
4750
(b)  |  Ethnography
and Area Studies:
ANTH1410, 2324, 2327, 2330, 2370, 2401, 2402, 2403, 2404,
2410, 2420, 2450, 2710, 2720, 2760, 3430, 3440, 3450, 3790
Total:  |  69
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  ANTH courses
at 2000 and above level as well as those labeled as # will be included in
the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Up to four related courses (12
units) offered by the Department of History and the Department of
Japanese Studies or up to three related courses (9 units) offered by
other Departments or Programmes (undergraduate or postgraduate) as
approved by this Department may be recognized as courses for fulfillment
of major programme requirement.
Voluntary Concentrations:
Students are encouraged to choose one,
or up to two concentrations by taking the relevant courses in fulfillment
of the Major Programme requirement listed above. Students will be
required to complete a minimum of five courses designated to the
concentration of their choice. (Other courses may also be recognized as
fulfillment of concentration requirement with the approval of the
Department.) The six concentrations are as follows:
(i)  |  Archaeology and Cultural Heritage
ANTH1710, 2370, 2710, 2720, 2730,
2740, 2760, 2810, 3770, 3780, 3790, 3820[2820], 4720, 4750
(ii)  |  Tourism and Cultural Representation
ANTH2230, 2520, 2730, 2760, 3370,
3770, 4220, 4720
(iii)  |  Globalization, Economy and Power
ANTH2324, 2380, 2420, 2510, 2520,
2530, 2540, 2550, 3340, 3350, 3370, 3380, 3450, 3540, 3770, 4210, 4330,
4410
(iv)  |  Chinese Cultures and Societies
ANTH1410, 2370, 2410, 2420, 2710,
2720, 2760, 3430, 3450
(v)  |  Values, Beliefs and Worldviews
ANTH2310, 2324, 2327, 2330, 2340,
2350, 2351, 2360, 2390, 2404, 2520, 2550, 3310, 3630, 4210
(vi)  |  Health and the Body
ANTH1310, 2320, 2321, 2351, 2380,
2390, 2810, 3350, 3360, 3390, 3820[2820], 4330, 4750
